title: S0/S2/G2: Definition must use the transfer system O nontrivially (no reduction to HHR)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Faithfulness gap (F1, definitional drift).** The problem demands a filtration *adapted to an incomplete transfer system* $\aO$. A solution that defines the generators/connectivity in a way that ignores $\aO$ and silently reproduces the Hill–Hopkins–Ravenel (complete) slice filtration answers the wrong question.

**To establish.**
- Specify *exactly* which slice cells / admissible norms / admissible $H$-sets enter the generating set, as an explicit function of the transfer system $\aO$ (equivalently its indexing system $\mathcal F^{\aO}_\bullet$).
- Make the dependence of the connectivity function $f_{\aO}(n,H)$ on $\aO$ explicit.
- **Boundary checks (mandatory):** at the *trivial* transfer system the construction must reduce to the Postnikov/Borel-type filtration; at the *complete* transfer system it must reduce to the HHR slice filtration. If either extreme is wrong, the definition is wrong.
- Note the first genuinely *incomplete* case occurs for $G$ with a nontrivial subgroup lattice (e.g. $C_{p^2}$, $C_{pq}$); $C_p$ has only the two extreme transfer systems.

Reference: Blumberg–Hill, *Operadic multiplications...norms and transfers*, Adv. Math. 2015 (indexing systems = transfer systems).
